result.markdown:
---
title: "Data Preprocessing"
subtitle: "Step 1: QC, alignment, and BAM processing"
sidebar: wes
---

## Overview

Data preprocessing prepares raw sequencing data for variant calling through quality control, alignment, and various BAM processing steps.

## Download Reference Files

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false

# GATK resource bundle (GRCh38)
# From Google Cloud or Broad FTP
gsutil -m cp -r gs://genomics-public-data/resources/broad/hg38/v0/* ./reference/

# Or download specific files
wget https://storage.googleapis.com/genomics-public-data/resources/broad/hg38/v0/Homo_sapiens_assembly38.fasta
wget https://storage.googleapis.com/genomics-public-data/resources/broad/hg38/v0/Homo_sapiens_assembly38.fasta.fai
wget https://storage.googleapis.com/genomics-public-data/resources/broad/hg38/v0/Homo_sapiens_assembly38.dict
```

## Quality Control

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false

# FastQC on raw reads
fastqc -t 8 -o fastqc_raw/ raw_data/*.fastq.gz

# Aggregate with MultiQC
multiqc fastqc_raw/ -o multiqc_raw/
```

## Adapter Trimming

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false

# Trim adapters with fastp
fastp \
    --in1 sample_R1.fastq.gz \
    --in2 sample_R2.fastq.gz \
    --out1 trimmed/sample_R1.fastq.gz \
    --out2 trimmed/sample_R2.fastq.gz \
    --html trimmed/sample_fastp.html \
    --json trimmed/sample_fastp.json \
    --thread 8 \
    --qualified_quality_phred 20 \
    --length_required 50 \
    --detect_adapter_for_pe
```

## Read Alignment

### BWA-MEM2 (Faster)

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false

# Index reference (one time)
bwa-mem2 index reference.fa

# Align reads
SAMPLE="sample1"
RGID="${SAMPLE}"
RGSM="${SAMPLE}"
RGPL="ILLUMINA"
RGLB="lib1"

bwa-mem2 mem -t 16 \
    -R "@RG\tID:${RGID}\tSM:${RGSM}\tPL:${RGPL}\tLB:${RGLB}" \
    reference.fa \
    trimmed/${SAMPLE}_R1.fastq.gz \
    trimmed/${SAMPLE}_R2.fastq.gz | \
    samtools sort -@ 8 -m 4G -o aligned/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam -

# Index BAM
samtools index aligned/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam
```

### Alternative: BWA-MEM

```{bash}
#| eval: false

bwa mem -t 16 -R "@RG\tID:sample\tSM:sample\tPL:ILLUMINA" \
    reference.fa sample_R1.fastq.gz sample_R2.fastq.gz | \
    samtools sort -@ 8 -o sample.sorted.bam -
```

## Mark Duplicates

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false

# Using GATK MarkDuplicates
gatk MarkDuplicates \
    -I aligned/sample.sorted.bam \
    -O aligned/sample.markdup.bam \
    -M aligned/sample.markdup_metrics.txt \
    --CREATE_INDEX true

# Or using sambamba (faster)
sambamba markdup -t 8 \
    aligned/sample.sorted.bam \
    aligned/sample.markdup.bam
```

## Base Quality Score Recalibration (BQSR)

BQSR corrects systematic errors in base quality scores.

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false

# Step 1: Build recalibration table
gatk BaseRecalibrator \
    -R reference.fa \
    -I aligned/sample.markdup.bam \
    --known-sites dbsnp_146.hg38.vcf.gz \
    --known-sites Mills_and_1000G_gold_standard.indels.hg38.vcf.gz \
    -O recal/sample.recal_data.table

# Step 2: Apply recalibration
gatk ApplyBQSR \
    -R reference.fa \
    -I aligned/sample.markdup.bam \
    --bqsr-recal-file recal/sample.recal_data.table \
    -O aligned/sample.bqsr.bam
```

## Alignment Quality Control

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false

# Collect multiple metrics
gatk CollectMultipleMetrics \
    -R reference.fa \
    -I aligned/sample.bqsr.bam \
    -O metrics/sample \
    --PROGRAM CollectAlignmentSummaryMetrics \
    --PROGRAM CollectInsertSizeMetrics \
    --PROGRAM CollectGcBiasMetrics

# Collect WES-specific metrics
gatk CollectHsMetrics \
    -R reference.fa \
    -I aligned/sample.bqsr.bam \
    -O metrics/sample.hs_metrics.txt \
    --BAIT_INTERVALS targets.interval_list \
    --TARGET_INTERVALS targets.interval_list

# Calculate coverage
mosdepth -t 4 -b targets.bed metrics/sample aligned/sample.bqsr.bam
```

### Key Quality Metrics

| Metric | Expected | Concern |
|--------|----------|---------|
| Mean target coverage | >80x | <50x |
| % bases ≥30x | >80% | <60% |
| % on-target reads | >70% | <50% |
| Duplicate rate | <20% | >30% |
| Insert size median | ~150-200 bp | Varies by library |

## Batch Processing Script

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false

#!/bin/bash
# Process multiple samples

REFERENCE="reference.fa"
DBSNP="dbsnp_146.hg38.vcf.gz"
MILLS="Mills_and_1000G_gold_standard.indels.hg38.vcf.gz"
THREADS=16

for R1 in raw_data/*_R1.fastq.gz; do
    SAMPLE=$(basename ${R1} _R1.fastq.gz)
    R2=${R1/_R1/_R2}
    
    echo "Processing: ${SAMPLE}"
    
    # Trim
    fastp --in1 ${R1} --in2 ${R2} \
          --out1 trimmed/${SAMPLE}_R1.fastq.gz \
          --out2 trimmed/${SAMPLE}_R2.fastq.gz \
          --thread 8
    
    # Align
    bwa-mem2 mem -t ${THREADS} \
        -R "@RG\tID:${SAMPLE}\tSM:${SAMPLE}\tPL:ILLUMINA" \
        ${REFERENCE} \
        trimmed/${SAMPLE}_R1.fastq.gz \
        trimmed/${SAMPLE}_R2.fastq.gz | \
        samtools sort -@ 8 -o aligned/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam -
    
    # Mark duplicates
    gatk MarkDuplicates \
        -I aligned/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam \
        -O aligned/${SAMPLE}.markdup.bam \
        -M metrics/${SAMPLE}.dup_metrics.txt \
        --CREATE_INDEX true
    
    # BQSR
    gatk BaseRecalibrator \
        -R ${REFERENCE} \
        -I aligned/${SAMPLE}.markdup.bam \
        --known-sites ${DBSNP} \
        --known-sites ${MILLS} \
        -O recal/${SAMPLE}.recal.table
    
    gatk ApplyBQSR \
        -R ${REFERENCE} \
        -I aligned/${SAMPLE}.markdup.bam \
        --bqsr-recal-file recal/${SAMPLE}.recal.table \
        -O aligned/${SAMPLE}.bqsr.bam
    
    echo "Completed: ${SAMPLE}"
done
```

## Next Steps

With preprocessed BAM files, proceed to:
- [SNV/Indel Calling](02-snv-calling.qmd)
- [CNV Calling](03-cnv-calling.qmd)
